title: Uncommon Bitter Portuguese Beef Stew
description: This stew features bitter notes from ferment-jang and a boost of flavor from beef stock concentrate, making it a show-off Portuguese dish, while keeping the glycemic index in check with low-glycemic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the beef cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the beef stock concentrate, ferment-jang, thyme, bay leaves, salt, and pepper, stirring to combine.
Add the chopped carrots and celery, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the bay leaves and serve the stew hot.
Let the stew cool, then refrigerate or freeze it for later use.
